_description: Declined Services Follow-Up — urgency tiers, follow-up cadence, offer templates

safety_critical.label: Safety Critical
safety_critical.tone: Urgent but not alarmist — focus on safety of driver and family
safety_critical.note: Do not offer a discount on safety items — it devalues the urgency. Focus on risk.

high.label: High Priority
high.tone: Concerned and helpful — explain risk of further delay

low.label: Low Priority
low.tone: Light touch — when you're ready, we're here

touch_1.label: First Follow-Up
touch_1.goal: Remind customer of declined service, explain why it matters

touch_2.label: Second Follow-Up
touch_2.goal: Add value — include discount offer or seasonal relevance

touch_3.label: Third Follow-Up
touch_3.goal: Final nudge — create mild urgency, reiterate risk

touch_4.label: Final Attempt (Safety Critical only)
touch_4.goal: Document outreach for liability purposes, invite customer to call
touch_4.note: After this touch, archive the declined item — do not continue outreach.

message_rules: Always name the specific service — never say 'work we recommended'.
Include the original estimate price in follow-up #1 and #2.
For safety items, describe the risk in plain language — not technical terms.
Never imply the customer made a bad decision for declining. Be understanding.
Always include a one-action CTA: call, reply, or click to book.
